title: Visualising roll distributions
text: roll_distribution() samples many rolls tallies often total comes . Passing result plot() draws bar chart counts across notation’s total range. Two six-sided dice give familiar triangular distribution, peaking 7.

what: A single roll against its distribution
title: Visualising roll distributions
text: plot() roll shows notation’s exact theoretical outcome distribution highlights bar total actually rolled. subtitle reports roll’s percentile standing, “beats P% outcomes” reading compare = TRUE print path uses. plot always shows theoretical distribution, whether roll created compare = TRUE.

what: Comparing a flat roll with a keep selector
title: Visualising roll distributions
text: Keep selectors change distribution’s shape. common example character-ability generation: 4d6h3 (roll four dice, keep highest three) skews higher flat 3d6, even though range 3 18. Sampling notation plotting results shows shift.
code: set.seed(42) plot(roll_distribution("3d6", n = 20000)) set.seed(42) plot(roll_distribution("4d6h3", n = 20000))

title: Visualising roll distributions
text: plot() method returns standard ggplot object, can capture keep composing +, example recolour bars drop subtitle.
code: set.seed(1) p <- plot(roll_distribution("2d6", n = 10000)) p +   geom_col(fill = "darkorange") +   labs(subtitle = NULL)

title: Get started with rollr2
text: roll() takes single notation string returns roll object. Printing shows notation, individual dice, adjusted total. roll object list. three fields use : dice: integer vector every die rolled. kept: dice contribute total (equal dice keep selector). total: sum kept dice plus modifier. notation carries keep selector, kept subset dice printout gains Kept: line. 4d6h3 rolls four six-sided dice keeps highest three.
code: set.seed(1) r <- roll("2d20+2") r #> <roll> 2d20+2 #> Dice:  4, 7 #> Total: 13 r$dice #> [1] 4 7 r$kept #> [1] 4 7 r$total #> [1] 13 set.seed(1) kept_roll <- roll("4d6h3") kept_roll #> <roll> 4d6h3 #> Dice:  1, 4, 1, 2 #> Kept:  4, 2, 1 #> Total: 7  kept_roll$dice #> [1] 1 4 1 2 kept_roll$kept #> [1] 4 2 1 kept_roll$total #> [1] 7

title: Get started with rollr2
text: roll_distribution() rolls whole notation n times summarises distribution totals. print method shows possible total range, count per observed outcome, text histogram. returned roll_distribution object stores observed totals counts, named integer vector whose names outcome totals (ascending, zero-count outcomes dropped), plus theoretical range possible totals.

title: Get started with rollr2
text: parser accepts following forms. leading d case-insensitive surrounding whitespace tolerated. keep selector follows die size comes modifier, full notation reads count, die size, optional keep selector, optional modifier, example 4d6h3+1. missing die count means one die (d6 1d6). missing modifier means adjustment (M = 0). missing keep count means keep one die (2d20h keeps single highest die). parser rejects notation outside bounds clear error: die count must positive integer. die size must integer least 2. keep count must 1 die count (keeping zero dice, dice rolled, rejected). Non-integer counts, die sizes, keep counts (2.5d6 2d6h1.5) parse.

title: Get started with rollr2
text: missing die count means one die (d6 1d6). missing modifier means adjustment (M = 0). missing keep count means keep one die (2d20h keeps single highest die).

title: Get started with rollr2
text: parser rejects notation outside bounds clear error: die count must positive integer. die size must integer least 2. keep count must 1 die count (keeping zero dice, dice rolled, rejected). Non-integer counts, die sizes, keep counts (2.5d6 2d6h1.5) parse.

what: Roll Tabletop RPG Dice from Notation
title: Roll Tabletop RPG Dice from Notation
text: rollr2 parses rolls tabletop RPG dice notation "2d20+2". single notation string reports every individual die, kept dice, adjusted total, can summarise outcome distribution across many repeated rolls.

title: Roll Tabletop RPG Dice from Notation
text: Install development version GitHub:

title: Roll Tabletop RPG Dice from Notation
text: roll() simulates single roll returns roll object holding individual dice total. functions stochastic, fix seed want reproducible output. keep selector like 4d6h3 keeps highest (h) lowest (l) dice; printout shows dice kept.
code: library(rollr2)  set.seed(1) roll("2d20+2") #> <roll> 2d20+2 #> Dice:  4, 7 #> Total: 13 set.seed(1) roll("4d6h3") #> <roll> 4d6h3 #> Dice:  1, 4, 1, 2 #> Kept:  4, 2, 1 #> Total: 7

title: Roll Tabletop RPG Dice from Notation
text: roll_distribution() rolls whole notation many times summarises distribution totals, printing count per outcome text histogram.

title: Roll Tabletop RPG Dice from Notation
text: See vignette("rollr2") get started, “Visualising roll distributions” article package website ggplot2 visualisations roll outcomes.

what: Roll dice from notation once — roll
title: Roll dice from notation once — roll
text: Parses dice-notation string simulates single roll: N independent uniform draws 1..X, summed adjusted modifier M. notation carries keep selector (h/l), highest lowest K rolled dice contribute total; modifier still applied kept sum.

title: Roll dice from notation once — roll
code: roll(notation, compare = FALSE)  # S3 method for class 'roll' print(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'roll' plot(x, ...)

title: Roll dice from notation once — roll
text: notation length-1 character string form NdX, NdX+M, NdX-M, count-omitted dX variants (case-insensitive d, whitespace-tolerant). optional keep selector h/l optional count may follow die size (e.g. 2d20h, 4d6h3, 3d6l2); keeps highest (h) lowest (l) K dice, defaulting K = 1. See roll_distribution() summarise many rolls. compare length-1 logical. TRUE, printing roll also shows total sits within notation's full theoretical outcome distribution: header line stating percent outcomes roll beats, text histogram exact outcome probabilities rolled total's bar marked. Defaults FALSE, prints roll . comparison computed, sampled, given (notation, total) pair always reports standing. x roll object, returned roll(). ... Ignored, compatibility print() plot() generics.

title: Roll dice from notation once — roll
text: roll object: list dice (integer vector length N, 1..X, listing every die rolled), total (integer scalar equal sum kept dice plus M), kept (kept dice, equal dice selector), parsed components n, x, m, keep, keep_n, original notation, compare (logical flag argument, controlling print method).

title: Roll dice from notation once — roll
text: plot() returns themed ggplot2::ggplot() bar chart notation's exact theoretical outcome distribution, rolled total's bar highlighted percentile standing shown subtitle. plot always shows theoretical distribution never reads compare, remains print-switch. returned object auto-prints called top level can captured extended +.

title: Roll dice from notation once — roll

what: Summarise the outcome distribution of repeated rolls — roll_distribution
title: Summarise the outcome distribution of repeated rolls — roll_distribution
text: Simulates n whole-notation rolls summarises distribution resulting totals. distribution sampled (computed analytically), results vary run run unless seed fixed set.seed(). keep selector (h/l) applied per simulated roll summing, total reflects kept dice plus modifier.

title: Summarise the outcome distribution of repeated rolls — roll_distribution
code: roll_distribution(notation, n)  # S3 method for class 'roll_distribution' print(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'roll_distribution' plot(x, ...)

title: Summarise the outcome distribution of repeated rolls — roll_distribution
text: notation length-1 character string form NdX, NdX+M, NdX-M, count-omitted dX variants (case-insensitive d, whitespace-tolerant). optional keep selector h/l optional count may follow die size (e.g. 2d20h, 4d6h3), keeping highest/lowest K dice per roll (defaulting K = 1). n Number whole-notation rolls simulate. positive integer. x roll_distribution object, returned roll_distribution(). ... Ignored, compatibility print() plot() generics.

title: Summarise the outcome distribution of repeated rolls — roll_distribution
text: roll_distribution object: list counts (integer vector observed totals, named outcome value, ordered ascending; zero-count outcomes omitted), range (theoretical c(min, max) total, c(K + M, K * X + M) K kept count, equal die count N selector), n, parsed components dice_n, x, m, keep, keep_n, original notation. print() method renders counts text histogram console.

title: Summarise the outcome distribution of repeated rolls — roll_distribution
text: plot() returns themed ggplot2::ggplot() bar chart sampled counts across notation's theoretical total range. returned object auto-prints called top level can captured extended +.

title: Summarise the outcome distribution of repeated rolls — roll_distribution

what: rollr2: Roll Tabletop RPG Dice from Notation — rollr2-package
title: rollr2: Roll Tabletop RPG Dice from Notation — rollr2-package
text: Parses rolls tabletop RPG dice notation "2d20+2", reporting individual die results, totals, outcome distribution across repeated rolls.

title: rollr2: Roll Tabletop RPG Dice from Notation — rollr2-package
text: Maintainer: Felix Mil felix.mil@esqlabs.com Authors: Felix Mil felix.mil@esqlabs.com

title: rollr2 0.0.0.9000
text: plot.roll() returns themed ggplot notation’s exact outcome distribution rolled total’s bar highlighted percentile standing subtitle; always shows theoretical distribution ignores compare flag. plot.roll_distribution() returns themed ggplot bar chart sampled totals across notation’s range. roll() rolls dice-notation string , returning individual die results total (sum dice plus modifier). roll() gains compare argument , TRUE, prints notation’s full outcome distribution text histogram rolled total’s bar marked header stating percent outcomes roll beats; standing computed exactly (never sampled), given notation total across sessions. Defaults FALSE, leaving existing output unchanged. roll_distribution() rolls dice-notation string many times summarises distribution totals, printing counts per outcome text histogram console. functions accept notation form NdX, NdX+M, NdX-M, count-omitted dX variants (case-insensitive d, whitespace-tolerant), plus optional keep selector h/l die size keeps highest lowest K dice (e.g. 2d20h, 4d6h3, 3d6l2, defaulting K = 1); reject invalid notation, non-positive non-integer die counts, die sizes 2, keep counts zero exceeding die count, non-positive-integer repetition counts clear errors.
